company-registration.title: China-Thailand Company Registration Services
company-registration.description: Professional cross-border company registration consulting for China-Thailand, including BOI investment promotion, foreign enterprise establishment, and equity structure design
company-registration.keywords: Thailand company registration,China-Thailand cross-border,BOI investment,foreign enterprise,equity structure
company-registration.heroDescription: From company type selection to registration completion, our AI legal system provides professional guidance throughout the process

faq.q1: What are the requirements for foreigners to register a company in Thailand?
faq.a1: Under Thailand's Foreign Business Act, foreigners can establish companies through BOI investment promotion, the Treaty of Amity, and other pathways. Specific requirements vary by company type.
faq.q2: How long does it take to register a company in Thailand?
faq.a2: Typically, the Thailand company registration process takes 2-4 weeks, including name approval, articles of association drafting, and registration filing.
faq.q3: Is there a minimum registered capital requirement?
faq.a3: Thai limited companies require a minimum registered capital of 5 million THB (if work permits are needed). BOI companies have different requirements based on project type.

visa-consultation.title: Thailand Visa Consultation Services
visa-consultation.description: Professional consultation for all Thailand visa types including work visas, business visas, Elite visas, retirement visas, and DTV digital nomad visas
visa-consultation.keywords: Thailand visa,work visa,business visa,Elite visa,retirement visa,DTV visa
visa-consultation.heroDescription: AI-powered matching for the best visa type for your situation, with complete application guidance and document checklists

faq.q1: What are the common visa types in Thailand?
faq.a1: Common visa types include Tourist visa, Non-B Business visa, Non-O Family visa, Elite visa, Retirement visa, Education visa, and DTV Digital Nomad visa.
faq.q2: What are the advantages of the Thailand Elite visa?
faq.a2: The Elite visa provides 5-20 years of long-term residency, eliminating frequent renewals, with VIP immigration lane access and other privileges.
faq.q3: What are the consequences of overstaying a visa?
faq.a3: Overstaying results in a fine of 500 THB per day (max 20,000 THB). Severe cases may lead to detention, deportation, and blacklisting.

contract-disputes.title: Cross-Border Contract Dispute Resolution
contract-disputes.description: Professional legal analysis for China-Thailand cross-border contract disputes, including contract review, risk assessment, dispute resolution strategies, and litigation/arbitration guidance
contract-disputes.keywords: cross-border contract disputes,contract review,dispute resolution,international arbitration,China-Thailand trade disputes
contract-disputes.heroDescription: AI-driven contract risk identification and dispute resolution strategies to protect your cross-border business interests

faq.q1: Should I choose litigation or arbitration for cross-border contract disputes?
faq.a1: International arbitration is usually preferred for cross-border disputes, as arbitral awards are more easily enforced across New York Convention signatory states.
faq.q2: What if the contract doesn't specify governing law?
faq.a2: If no governing law is specified, courts or tribunals will determine the applicable law based on the closest connection principle.
faq.q3: What is the statute of limitations for cross-border contract disputes?
faq.a3: Limitation periods vary by applicable law. China generally has 3 years, Thailand's civil limitation is generally 10 years, depending on the case type.

labor-law.title: China-Thailand Labor Law Consulting
labor-law.description: Professional labor law consulting for China and Thailand, covering employment contracts, work permits, social security, and labor disputes
labor-law.keywords: Thailand labor law,work permit,employment contract,social security,labor disputes
labor-law.heroDescription: Comprehensive understanding of labor regulations in both countries to ensure employer compliance and employee rights protection

faq.q1: What restrictions apply to hiring foreign employees in Thailand?
faq.a1: Thailand requires a foreign-to-Thai employee ratio not exceeding 1:4, and all foreign employees must hold valid work permits.
faq.q2: What is the minimum wage in Thailand?
faq.a2: Thailand's minimum daily wage varies by region. In Bangkok, it is approximately 353 THB/day, with slight variations in other areas.
faq.q3: What does Thai labor law say about termination?
faq.a3: Thailand's Labor Protection Act requires employers to provide advance notice or pay in lieu of notice. Employees with sufficient tenure are entitled to severance pay.

tax-advisory.title: China-Thailand Tax Advisory Services
tax-advisory.description: Professional cross-border tax consulting for China-Thailand, including double taxation agreements, transfer pricing, tax compliance, and tax planning
tax-advisory.keywords: China-Thailand tax,double taxation agreement,transfer pricing,tax compliance,cross-border tax planning
tax-advisory.heroDescription: Optimize tax structures using the China-Thailand Double Taxation Agreement to ensure cross-border tax compliance

faq.q1: Is there a double taxation agreement between China and Thailand?
faq.a1: Yes, China and Thailand have signed an Agreement for the Avoidance of Double Taxation, effectively preventing the same income from being taxed by both countries.
faq.q2: What taxes apply to businesses operating in Thailand?
faq.a2: Main taxes include corporate income tax (20%), VAT (7%), withholding tax, and stamp duty. Specific rates vary by business type.
faq.q3: How is personal income tax calculated for foreigners in Thailand?
faq.a3: Foreigners residing in Thailand for more than 180 days are taxed on worldwide income at progressive rates of 5%-35%.

ip-protection.title: Intellectual Property Protection Services
ip-protection.description: Cross-border IP protection consulting for China-Thailand, covering trademark registration, patent applications, copyright protection, and IP enforcement
ip-protection.keywords: IP protection,trademark registration,patent application,copyright protection,cross-border IP
ip-protection.heroDescription: AI-assisted IP risk assessment and protection strategies to safeguard your innovations

faq.q1: How long does trademark registration take in Thailand?
faq.a1: Thailand trademark registration typically takes 12-18 months, including formal examination, substantive examination, and publication period.
faq.q2: Is a Chinese trademark protected in Thailand?
faq.a2: Trademark protection is territorial. A trademark registered in China is not automatically protected in Thailand and requires separate registration.
faq.q3: What should I do if my IP rights are infringed?
faq.a3: You can pursue administrative complaints, civil litigation, or criminal prosecution. It is recommended to collect evidence of infringement and consult a professional lawyer first.
